assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: Response includes specific command to check for deprecated API warnings: `kubectl get --raw /metrics | grep deprecated`

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions checking custom admission webhooks in the validation checklist and notes they need to be functioning

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: Response discusses API server resource limits and includes commands to check resource usage on control plane nodes and system components

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: Response includes multiple commands to check kube-system pods status and mentions looking for control plane pods crash-looping as an escalation trigger

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: Response includes specific commands using `kubectl get --raw /metrics` to check API server metrics, duration, and request totals

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: Response includes sections on CNI/network plugin changes, service mesh impact checks, and network policy validation in the checklist

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: Response includes commands to check for readiness probe failures and pod events, specifically `kubectl get events -A --field-selector reason=Unhealthy`

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: Response does not mention HPA (Horizontal Pod Autoscaler) or VPA (Vertical Pod Autoscaler) components or checking their behavior after the upgrade
